name: aurora-soft
description: A bright, light SaaS analytics dashboard where saturated color lives only inside the data — an indigo→violet→blue→teal gradient spectrum in charts — while the chrome stays neutral white-on-cool-grey.
author: getdrip
signature_traits: Saturated color appears ONLY inside charts (the four-stop spectrum #5347CE/#887CFD/#4896FE/#16C8C7); the chrome carries no saturated color except the single indigo accent.
Indigo #5347CE is the only interactive color in the entire UI — there is no second chrome accent, and spectrum hues never touch a button, link, border, or background.
Bar charts use the focal-bar pattern: the bar in focus gets the gradient fill, every other bar falls back to #F5F6F8 grey.
Cards are flat white on a cool-grey #EBEEF2 canvas, separated by a 1px #E6E8EE hairline plus one 4%-opacity shadow — never colored backgrounds, never heavy drop shadows.
Trend deltas render as fully-rounded tinted pills (green or rose soft background) prefixed with ↗ / ↘ arrow glyphs — never bare colored text, never triangles.
Numbers use SF Pro Display with font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums — there is no separate monospace font; tabular figures do the anti-jitter job instead.
Metric-to-label hierarchy is fixed at 2:1, reinforced by three stacked signals: larger size, SemiBold weight, and primary contrast on the value vs Medium secondary on the label.
The sidebar is white like the cards with a hairline divider, and the active nav item uses an #EDEBFB lavender soft-fill — never a solid indigo block — so navigation recedes below content.
Spacing runs three explicit density tiers in one view: 24px KPI cards, 20px panels, 44px table rows — never uniform padding.
Recharts defaults are always overridden: custom white tooltip, hidden Y-axis, horizontal gridlines only, spectrum gradient fills — never the library's default palette or vertical gridlines.
